summary: Replaced several already-matching inline ASM fallbacks in src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c with C implementations for ftMaterial_800BFB4C, ftMaterial_800BFD04, and ftMaterial_800BFD9C, deleting a large amount of guarded assembly while keeping the matched C paths. The PR is a cleanup/matching consolidation: it trusts existing matches and removes redundant #if MUST_MATCH assembly bodies.
what_changed: Changed ftMaterial_800BFB4C from an ASM function with C guarded by !MUST_MATCH || WIP into a plain C function.
Changed ftMaterial_800BFD04 from an ASM function with an assembly fallback into a plain C function.
Changed ftMaterial_800BFD9C from an ASM function with an assembly fallback into a plain C function.
Deleted the inline PowerPC assembly implementations for ftMaterial_800BFB4C, ftMaterial_800BFD04, and ftMaterial_800BFD9C.
Simplified ftMaterial_800BFB4C's local C slightly by using ternary null checks for dobj->mobj and dobj->next and by checking mobj->mat before assigning mat->diffuse.
Net change was small C edits plus a large reduction in assembly: 8 lines added and 274 deleted in one file.
smart_moves: Removed redundant assembly only after existing C versions were apparently known to match, reducing maintenance burden without introducing new algorithmic work.

Kept the C behavior close to the old generated/control-flow shape in ftMaterial_800BFB4C, including explicit null-safe traversal of JObj and DObj lists, which is useful for preserving matching-sensitive codegen.
Converted three neighboring ftmaterial routines together, making the file more consistently C-based in this address range.

Used existing typed accessors and macros such as GET_JOBJ, GET_FIGHTER, HSD_JObjGetDObj, HSD_JObjGetFlags, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, Player_GetEntityAtIndex, and gm_80167320 instead of keeping raw offset-oriented assembly.
decomp_lessons: When a C implementation is already matching under non-MUST_MATCH/WIP guards, the next cleanup step is to remove the ASM wrapper and fallback rather than repeatedly carrying both sources of truth.

For traversal-heavy HSD object code, matching C may need explicit null-preserving ternaries even inside loops where the current pointer is expected to be non-null; this can mirror the compiler's defensive branch pattern.
Bitfield-backed fighter flags such as x221F_b1 and x221F_b4 can replace raw rlwimi/lbz/stb sequences once the relevant Fighter struct fields are understood.

Calls to high-level fighter/common functions can collapse sizable assembly bodies when the surrounding constants and field names are known: ftMaterial_800BFD04 is expressed as Fighter_ChangeMotionState plus flag updates, and ftMaterial_800BFD9C reuses ftMaterial_800BFD04 before companion/player side effects.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based unresolved function names: ftMaterial_800BFB4C, ftMaterial_800BFD04, ftMaterial_800BFD9C.
Used project subsystem prefix casing ftMaterial_ for material routines and ftCo_ for common fighter symbols such as ftCo_GObj and ftCo_MS_Sleep.
Used HSD type prefixes for graphics scene graph/material types: HSD_JObj, HSD_DObj, HSD_MObj, HSD_Material, HSD_TExp.
Used Fighter struct bitfield-style names visible in the diff/search terms, including x221F_b1 and x221F_b4, where semantic names were not yet established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted guarded C matches by removing ASM/no-fralloc bodies and #if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP) scaffolding.
Preserved matching-sensitive null checks with expressions like dobj != NULL ? dobj->mobj : NULL and dobj != NULL ? dobj->next : NULL.
Replaced raw material color store assembly with typed assignment mat->diffuse = *diffuse.
Reused ftMaterial_800BFD04 inside ftMaterial_800BFD9C rather than duplicating the motion-state and flag-setting sequence, matching the existing C implementation shown in the diff.
Removed many labels from the inline assembly fallback, including lbl_800BFB6C through lbl_800BFE48, because the C implementations were accepted as matches.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The slice records one review but provides no review body or actionable reviewer feedback, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftMaterial_800BFB4C, ftMaterial_800BFD04, or ftMaterial_800BFD9C to semantic names.
Search for other ftmaterial.c functions still using ASM guarded by MUST_MATCH/WIP that may already have matching C implementations.
Look for similar HSD_JObj/HSD_DObj traversal code to reuse the same null-checking pattern for matching.
Audit Fighter bitfields around x221F_b1 and x221F_b4 for later semantic documentation or renames.
